channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE access to US intelligence secrets poses a national security threat, Democrats say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic lawmakers are demanding answers from Elon Musk about whether staffers at his Department of Government Efficiency have shared national security secrets over insecure communication channels. Sen. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts and Rep.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Andrew Tate, who faces rape and trafficking charges in Romania, has left for the US
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, left for the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions imposed as part of the case, an official said Thursday. The brothers — who are dual U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navy stops asking personnel about gender identity, makes 'intimate spaces' either male or female
item.description: The U.S. Navy has joined other military branches in making changes and defining two sexes as a result of an executive order signed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wife of murdered Minnesota pastor charged in alleged plot to kill husband on mission trip: church
item.description: Church officials say the wife of a Minnesota missionary is officially facing charges in his killing of her husband in a murder-to-hire plot in Angola.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope continues slow recovery from double pneumonia
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What you need to know about Adnan Syed's murder conviction
item.description: The legal battle over Adnan Syed 's conviction, scrutinized a decade ago in the hit podcast “Serial,” keeps twisting and turning, even after prosecutors freed him from more than 23 years in prison for a murder he still says he didn't commit.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: John-Paul Miller accuser's lawyer: Mica's death triggered guilt
item.description: John-Paul Miller had served as a pastor at Solid Rock Church but was released after his wife’s death.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Vote expected on Iowa bill that would remove gender identity protections from civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa lawmakers are expected to vote Thursday on an unprecedented bill that would strip the state civil rights code of protections based on gender identity, a move that opponents say could expose transgender people to discrimination in employment, public accommodations, housin
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Judge weighs continued freedom for Adnan Syed in 'Serial' murder case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — More than a decade after Adnan Syed’s murder case attracted massive interest through the hit podcast “Serial,” a Baltimore judge is considering a request to reduce his sentence to time served, which would allow him to remain free indefinitely even though his conviction still stands.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Democratic governors pitch state jobs to federal workers cut by Trump
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Some of America's governors — mostly Democrats — have a message for the wave of fired federal workers: We want you.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NFLPA report cards: Dolphins, Vikings finish at the top for second straight year
item.description: The Miami Dolphins are the top-ranked team, followed by the Minnesota Vikings, for the second consecutive season in the NFL Players Association report card.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Recent aviation disasters and close calls stoke fears about the safety of flying
item.description: All the recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington D.C. last month was the worst disaster.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, WGN 9

item.title: USAID employees given 15 minutes to clear out offices
item.description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees were notified late Tuesday they would be given 15 minutes to collect their belongings from their offices as the Trump administration forges ahead with gutting the agency.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump pitches $5 million 'gold card' as replacement for investor visa
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told reporters in the Oval Office that the Trump administration would be ending the existing EB5 visa program, while President Trump boasted the new visa would bolster the economy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: Unvaccinated child dies in measles outbreak that infected over 120 people
item.description: A person who was hospitalized with measles has died from measles in West Texas, the first death in an outbreak that began late last month.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams claims prosecutorial misconduct, asking judge to dismiss criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams asked a federal judge to toss out the corruption case against him Wednesday, alleging prosecutorial misconduct, even as the Justice Department seeks dismissal of the charges on the Democrat's behalf.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas leads nation in housing ICE detainees
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facilities in Texas are holding the most immigrants, according to new data.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dragons to fly over Universal’s ‘How To Train Your Dragon’ land
item.description: Walking and flying dragons will greet guests as they explore How to Train Your Dragon – Isle of Berk, one of the five immersive lands that will be featured in the all-new Universal Epic Universe set to open on May 22. The theme park resort teased the news in a blog post released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump suspends security clearances for Jack Smith's outside lawyers
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memo suspending security clearances for a number of outside lawyers who are defending former special counsel Jack Smith in his personal capacity. White House staff secretary Will Scharf said in the Oval Office that the memo targeted Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court schedules arguments in case where Trump administration is defending ACA
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday scheduled arguments for April 21 in a case that could decide the legality of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) requirement that insurers cover certain preventive services.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Come 'Beyond the Gates' for the first Black-led soap opera
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first thing viewers of “Beyond the Gates” see is a black Mercedes gliding past manicured lawns and stately estates. The sedan pulls up at an elegant country club and the valets come out. The Emotions' funky tune “Best of My Love” is playing.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4
